event_preview:
Indianapolis Motor Speedway is a 2.5-mile (4.023 km) D-shaped oval in Speedway, Indiana, built in 1909 and opened for racing in 1911. The 110th Running of the Indianapolis 500 is scheduled for Sunday, May 24, 2026, at 12:45 PM ET. Alex Palou is the defending Indianapolis 500 champion, having claimed victory in 2025, and enters the race as the series championship leader with three wins already this season.

Two-time recent Indianapolis 500 winner Josef Newgarden is among the championship contenders, having won the race in two of the last three years — his form at this venue unmatched by any active driver save Palou. Katherine Legge will attempt the Indianapolis 500 / Coca-Cola 600 "Double," racing here before flying to Charlotte for the NASCAR Cup Series race later that Sunday. The field features multiple drivers seeking their first Indianapolis 500 victory, including IndyCar rookies and established series veterans alike.

The all-time pole-winning record at Indianapolis belongs to Scott McLaughlin, who averaged 234.220 mph (376.940 km/h) over four laps on 19 May 2024. The fastest individual lap ever recorded in Indianapolis 500 history is Eddie Cheever's 236.103 mph in the 1996 race.
